description: A vertical, portrait-orientation graphic from USCIS. The top half is a color photograph of identity documents spread on a white surface: a dark blue passport (lettered 'PASSPORT'), an open Australian passport photo page with a man's photo and machine-readable 'AUS<<<' code, an Australian ID and driver-license card with a gold EMV chip, several loose passport-style headshot photos of a bearded man, and an open pair of metal scissors lying across the documents. The bottom half is a solid dark navy panel containing the circular DHS Department of Homeland Security seal (eagle crest) above 'U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services' in grey serif type. Below, the headline in bold yellow and cream serif reads 'Providing a fake document to USCIS is fraud,' and beneath it in bold white sans-serif: 'Fraud can get you permanently banned from the U.S.'
notable_text: Providing a fake document to USCIS is fraud; Fraud can get you permanently banned from the U.S.
